defects.symptom: Build's checklist-item→agent matcher mis-assigns specialists: a code-implementation task containing 'comment added' routes to the docs specialist; an explicit pytest item ties implement-vs-test and the tie-break hands it to the implementer; a docs-update item routes to the implementer over the docs specialist. Reproduced at HEAD with `map_task_to_equipment` against a realistic post-equip manifest: ev15's SQL/service item -> backend-docs-specialist (fallback=False); 'add a pytest scan' -> python-implementer instead of python-tester; 'Docs updated: ... (mount ...)' -> python-implementer over backend-docs-specialist. (Evidence items 15, 49, residual of 3.)
locations: dummyindex/context/domains/buildloop/mapping.py:86-89 (DOCS lexicon includes the generic token 'comment' — violating the module's own line 41-42 'keep generic words OUT' rule)

dummyindex/context/domains/buildloop/mapping.py:43-52 (IMPLEMENT lexicon has only base/3rd-person forms — 'add'/'adds' but not 'added'; 'update'/'updates' but not 'updated'; no 'gains'/'created' — so file-path+contract phrased code items often score 0 on IMPLEMENT while a single generic specialist token wins 1-0)

dummyindex/context/domains/buildloop/mapping.py:165-175 (strict `score > best_score` means ties go to the FIRST manifest item; equip always writes the implementer first per catalog.py:77-107, so any implement-vs-specialist tie lands on the implementer — confirmed cause of the pytest item mis-route)
dummyindex/context/domains/equip/generate/catalog.py:77-107 (manifest order: implementer/tester/reviewer first, specialists appended)
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED by running the mapper at HEAD. Three concrete mechanisms in mapping.py: (1) the DOCS capability lexicon contains 'comment' — a word ordinary code tasks use — so one incidental token outscores a code item that hits zero IMPLEMENT tokens; (2) the IMPLEMENT lexicon lacks past-tense/inflected verb forms ('added', 'updated', 'gains', 'created'), so realistic plan-task phrasing scores 0 against the implementer and any single specialist-token hit wins; (3) ties break on manifest order via the strict `>` comparison at mapping.py:171, and the generic implementer is always first in the manifest, so test/db/docs items that ALSO contain an implement verb ('add a pytest scan') are confidently routed to the implementer.
defects.proposed_fix: Surgical, all in mapping.py, keeping the deterministic no-LLM contract: (a) remove 'comment' (and consider 'docstring') from the DOCS lexicon at mapping.py:86-89; (b) extend the IMPLEMENT lexicon with the inflected forms tasks actually write: 'added', 'updated', 'created', 'gains', 'renamed', 'moved' (the lexicon comment at lines 38-41 already says to include inflections); (c) change the tie-break so that on equal non-zero scores a SPECIALIST (any item whose capabilities are not just implement/review — i.e. test, database, docs, security, performance, search, frontend, data) outranks the generic implement-capable item; among specialists, manifest order still breaks ties (stable, deterministic). Implementation: track ties explicitly instead of strict `>` — when `score == best_score and score > 0`, prefer the candidate that is not implement-capable over one that is. Update the module docstring (mapping.py:11-12) and models.py Choice doc accordingly. Design note for implementer: do NOT add a stemmer — keep exact-token matching; just enrich the lexicon and fix the tie policy.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/domains/test_build_loop.py: (1) test_code_task_with_comment_added_routes_to_implementer — ev15's exact item text + manifest with docs specialist + implementer asserts implementer wins; (2) test_pytest_item_beats_implement_tie — 'add a pytest scan ...' with implementer-first manifest asserts python-tester; (3) test_docs_update_item_routes_to_docs_specialist — 'Docs updated: `.context/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` (mount + ...)' asserts docs specialist over implementer; (4) regression test that pure code items (ev0 #9 text) still route to the implementer (default rule intact).

defects.symptom: GATE/decision checklist items (e.g. '**GATE** — ... Decide the RLS/tenant-isolation model ... Blocks all DDL') are emitted by `build --next/--next-wave` as confident agent dispatches (subagent_type python-implementer or backend-db-specialist, fallback=false, equipped=true). A decision the USER must make is offered as a dispatchable subagent item; per the build skill's wave discipline the whole wave then blocks at verify time instead of being resolved up front. Reproduced at HEAD: ev39's GATE text -> backend-db-specialist (fallback=False) with a db specialist in the manifest, python-implementer without one; ev49's decision GATE -> python-implementer. (Evidence items 39, 49; focus claim 5's interactive-item half.)
locations: dummyindex/context/domains/buildloop/checklist.py:41-84 (parse_checklist has no notion of item kind — every `- [ ]` line is a uniform work item)
dummyindex/context/domains/buildloop/models.py:25-31 (ChecklistItem carries only index/text/done/group)
dummyindex/cli/build_loop/waves.py:86-104 (_entry_for unconditionally maps every item to an agent + subagent_type)
dummyindex/skills/plan/SKILL.md:69-93 (checklist derivation never defines a GATE/decision convention, though real proposals contain '**GATE**' items)
dummyindex/skills/build/SKILL.md:43 (a 'decision the user must make' is only discovered as a blocking failure AFTER dispatch/verify)
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED by code reading + running the mapper: there is no GATE/decision recognition anywhere in the buildloop domain or CLI (grep for 'gate' hits only wave-gating prose). The mapper happily keyword-scores the gate's text ('migrations', 'sql', 'rls') and binds a specialist or, via the v0.16 default rule, the implementer — with fallback=False signalling a confident, equipped match. The plan skill never tells the planner to mark or isolate decision items, so they land inside parallel waves where they block siblings.
defects.proposed_fix: Three coordinated changes: (1) domain — recognize a leading GATE marker in item text (suggested regex `^\**GATE\**\s*[—:-]`, tolerant of bold markdown) and add a frozen `is_gate: bool = False` field to ChecklistItem (set in parse_checklist) — immutable dataclass extension, default keeps back-compat; (2) CLI — in waves.py `_entry_for`, when `item.is_gate` emit `"gate": true` with `agent`/`subagent_type` set to null (JSON) and a human line like 'GATE — requires a user decision; resolve before dispatching this wave' instead of an agent line; (3) skills — build SKILL.md: add a step-3 rule 'a `gate: true` item is never dispatched: surface the decision to the user FIRST, record the answer, tick the gate, then dispatch the remaining wave items'; plan SKILL.md step 8: document the GATE convention and require each GATE item be a singleton wave placed before the work it blocks. DESIGN DECISION for implementer: the exact marker syntax (recommend matching what sessions already write — leading 'GATE' with em-dash/colon) and whether mapping.py also needs the flag (recommend doing it at parse time only, keeping the mapper unaware).
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/domains/test_build_loop.py: test_gate_item_parsed_with_is_gate (parse_checklist on '- [ ] **GATE** — Decide ...'), test_gate_item_entry_has_no_agent (CLI-level via the run()/do_next path on a tmp proposal dir: JSON payload has gate=true, subagent_type=None), test_plain_item_not_gate (text merely containing the word 'gate' mid-sentence is not flagged), test_gate_inside_wave_still_listed (wave frontier includes the gate so the conductor sees it first).

defects.symptom: equip --for-proposal adopted 'Frontend Developer' as the covering specialist for a pure-backend FastAPI repo on a security/auth-heavy MCP proposal — a garbage manifest entry (grounded_in=[], path='') that Claude refused to route work to and that later had to be hand-removed. Reproduced at HEAD: `capabilities_from_text('... tools serving clients building UI dashboards')` extracts FRONTEND from one stray 'UI' word; frontend has no specialist template, so resolve_coverage falls through to the registry and adopts 'Frontend Developer' with zero consultation of the detected stack (python/FastAPI, no frontend at all). (Evidence items 1, 36's pollution half, 38 adjacent.)
locations: dummyindex/context/domains/equip/constants.py:56 (FRONTEND proposal tokens: 'frontend', 'ui', 'css', 'react' — one whole-word 'ui' anywhere in plan.md/checklist.md fires the capability)
dummyindex/context/domains/equip/generate/proposal.py:39-60 (extraction is pure text; no stack awareness; prefix matching at line 60)
dummyindex/context/domains/equip/generate/adopt.py:43-50 (_REGISTRY_CAPABILITIES maps frontend -> SubagentType.FRONTEND 'Frontend Developer')

dummyindex/context/domains/equip/generate/adopt.py:113-129 (resolve_coverage: project-agent -> template -> registry; frontend is NOT templated (specialists.py:74-131 — db/security/perf/docs/search only), so it always reaches the registry; the function never receives or consults the StackProfile)
dummyindex/context/domains/equip/generate/catalog.py:62-67 (build_catalog HAS the profile in scope but passes proposal_capabilities through unfiltered)
dummyindex/cli/equip/dispatch.py:220-248 (CLI call site: extract_proposal_capabilities then build_catalog(profile=...))
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED live at HEAD by executing extract_proposal_capabilities + reading resolve_coverage: proposal capability extraction is stack-blind keyword matching, frontend is deliberately non-templated ('frontend stays adopt-fallback'), and the registry adoption path has no stack-consistency check — so any backend proposal whose prose mentions 'UI' (e.g. MCP tools 'for clients building UI') adopts the 'Frontend Developer' registry persona into equipment.json for a repo with zero frontend code. (The companion claim that security was missed despite RLS criticals is FIXED at HEAD — see not_confirmed.)
defects.proposed_fix: Gate frontend adoption on detected stack evidence, in build_catalog (catalog.py) where the StackProfile is already in scope — keeps resolve_coverage pure and its signature stable: before calling resolve_coverage, filter Capability.FRONTEND out of proposal_capabilities unless the profile shows frontend evidence (profile.label in a frontend-ish set e.g. {'typescript','javascript'} OR any of profile.frameworks in {'React','Vue','Svelte','Next', ...}). Emit nothing for the dropped capability (the generic implementer covers it), or — better for transparency — have the CLI print 'skipped frontend adoption: no frontend stack detected' in the non-json path. DESIGN DECISION: (a) filter location — catalog-level (recommended, surgical) vs threading profile into resolve_coverage; (b) whether 'ui' should be removed from _PROPOSAL_CAPABILITY_TOKENS frontend row entirely (it is the only token that fires on backend prose; 'frontend'/'react'/'css' are unambiguous) — recommend removing 'ui' AND adding the stack gate, since the gate also protects the 'react'-prefix path ('reaction' etc. via the startswith match).
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/domains/equip/test_equip_catalog.py: test_frontend_capability_dropped_for_backend_stack (profile label='python', frameworks=('FastAPI',), proposal_capabilities containing 'frontend' -> decision.adopt has no 'Frontend Developer'); test_frontend_adopted_when_stack_is_frontend (label='typescript', frameworks=('React',) -> adoption preserved). tests/context/domains/equip/test_equip.py or a new test in test_equip_adopt.py: regression that capabilities_from_text on backend MCP prose with 'UI' no longer yields a frontend adoption end-to-end through build_catalog.

defects.symptom: '— via <tool>' tags on checklist items are treated as soft hints: the CLI never parses or surfaces them (the tag is invisible in --next/--next-wave output except as raw item text), and the shipped build skill's 'directive, not a hint' wording carries no failure semantics — so build agents hand-wrote the output the tagged tool (canvas-to-code bridge) was supposed to produce, shipping a no-source UI that was later reverted (commit b395ce64 in the user's repo). The user hand-patched BOTH the in-repo and ~/.claude copies of the build skill to add 'BINDING routing / substitution is a build failure / provenance gate' — patches the next `dummyindex install` will clobber because the fix never reached the shipped skill source. (C10 evidence items 14/28/30; focus claim 4; ev15's via-tag guess.)
locations: dummyindex/skills/build/SKILL.md:36 (the shipped 'Honor — via <tool> tags' paragraph: routing guidance only — nothing about what happens when the tool is unavailable, errors, or an agent substitutes hand-written output)
dummyindex/context/domains/buildloop/mapping.py (no via parsing — confirmed by grep over the whole buildloop domain + cli/build_loop)
dummyindex/cli/build_loop/waves.py:86-104 (_entry_for emits index/text/agent/subagent_type/fallback only — no `via` field for JSON consumers)
dummyindex/skills/plan/SKILL.md:46 (defines the tag but with no binding/verification contract for build time)
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED: the via tag exists only as prose inside item text. The deterministic layer (mapping/waves) is via-blind, so the tag also pollutes keyword scoring (e.g. '— via /code-review' contributes a 'review' token), and the conductor has no machine-readable signal to enforce. The shipped skill instruction stops at 'route the item through that tool' — it never states the substitution-is-failure rule, so under pressure an agent 'completes' the item by hand and the verify step (which checks the spec, not tool provenance) passes it. Silently-wrong results reached the user's repo before being caught.
defects.proposed_fix: Upstream the user's hardening into the SHIPPED sources (direction matters — repo first, installs follow): (1) CLI — parse a trailing `\s+—\s+via\s+(\S+)$` tag off the item text in `_entry_for` (or a small helper in buildloop/checklist.py so it is testable in the domain), emit it as a `via` field in both --next and --next-wave JSON + a `via:` line in human output, and strip the tag's tokens from the text passed to map_task_to_equipment so it cannot skew keyword scoring; (2) skills/build/SKILL.md step 3 — replace the soft paragraph with the binding contract: '— via tags are BINDING routing, not a hint. Substitution is a build failure: if the tagged tool is unavailable, errors, or cannot complete, leave the item UNTICKED, STOP, and report — never let an agent hand-write the output the tool was supposed to produce.' Plus a generic provenance rule in step 4 (verify): 'for a via-tagged item, verify the TOOL actually ran from its own evidence (its state files / command output), not the agent's claim'. Keep the canvas-to-code-specific state-file path OUT of the generic skill — express it as 'tools that record run state must show a run for this item'. DESIGN DECISION: whether a via-skill item should skip agent matching entirely and be marked conductor-executed (interacts with defect D5) — recommend yes for `— via <plugin>:<command>` (slash-commands a subagent cannot run) and no for `— via /<skill>` (a subagent can invoke skills).
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/domains/test_build_loop.py: test_via_tag_parsed_into_entry (item '... — via vercel:deploy' -> JSON entry carries via='vercel:deploy' and text scoring excluded the tag), test_via_tag_does_not_skew_matching ('Review the diff — via /code-review' vs the same text untagged map identically once the tag is stripped), test_next_wave_json_includes_via_field. Skill-text assertions: extend the existing skill-doc sync test (tests/cli/test_cli_doc_sync.py pattern) or add a simple content test that build/SKILL.md contains the 'Substitution is a build failure' sentence so future edits can't silently soften it.

defects.symptom: Non-dispatchable equipment manifest entries compete in the build matcher and are emitted as dispatchable: marketplace plugins are recorded as kind:'agent' with subagent_type=None (discover.py), generated skills (e.g. {proj}-verify) carry test/verify capabilities, and vendored skills carry capabilities too — any of them can WIN the keyword match for a checklist item, producing entries like agent='<plugin>@<marketplace>', subagent_type='general-purpose', fallback=false. The conductor is told a skill-only plugin is the matched 'agent' for an item it cannot dispatch as a subagent. (Focus claim 5's plugin half; evidence item 37 adjacent.)
locations: dummyindex/cli/build_loop/waves.py:48-68 (_load_manifest keeps every item dict regardless of kind)
dummyindex/context/domains/buildloop/mapping.py:143-199 (map_task_to_equipment never inspects 'kind' or requires a truthy subagent_type — confirmed: the word 'kind' does not appear in the module)
dummyindex/cli/equip/discover.py:391-409 (_record_native stamps every installed plugin kind=EquipmentKind.AGENT, subagent_type unset)
dummyindex/context/domains/equip/generate/catalog.py:100-106 ({proj}-verify SKILL carries TEST+VERIFY capabilities)
dummyindex/context/domains/equip/plugins/vendor.py:42 (vendored items get kind skill/agent with capabilities)
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED by code reading: the matcher treats the manifest as a flat list of agent candidates. Equipment kinds exist as a closed enum (EquipmentKind in equip/enums.py) but the buildloop layer never filters on them, and equip's plugin installer mis-records plugins as kind 'agent' anyway. A matched non-agent yields fallback=False with the generic-purpose literal substituted at waves.py:102 — a confident-looking but wrong dispatch instruction.
defects.proposed_fix: Two-part, build-side only (the manifest-schema correction for plugin kind belongs to the C2/C4 owners — coordinate): (1) in map_task_to_equipment (or in _load_manifest, simpler), only entries with kind == 'agent' (tolerant: missing kind treated as agent for v1 back-compat) AND a truthy subagent_type participate as dispatch candidates — the implement-capable default route gets the same filter; (2) instead of discarding the signal, surface a matched non-agent as a `via` suggestion on the entry (ties into D4's `via` field): if a skill/command/plugin entry scores highest, emit it as `via_suggestion` alongside the best dispatchable agent so the conductor can route through the tool. DESIGN DECISION: whether v1 manifest entries lacking subagent_type (generated pre-v2 agents) should still be dispatch candidates via their name — recommend yes when kind=='agent' (waves.py already falls back to general-purpose for them), i.e. filter only on kind, and treat subagent_type=None+kind=agent as today.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/domains/test_build_loop.py: test_skill_item_never_wins_dispatch (manifest with {kind:'skill', name:'proj-verify', capabilities:['test','verify']} + implementer; item 'verify the smoke test' routes to a dispatchable agent, not the skill), test_marketplace_plugin_not_a_dispatch_candidate (kind:'agent' source:'marketplace' subagent_type:None named 'canvas-to-code@acme' with frontend caps must not be emitted as the agent for a UI item once the kind/subagent rules land — pin whichever rule the implementer chooses), test_v1_agent_entry_still_matches (kind absent -> still a candidate).

defects.symptom: Hardcoded global persona rosters are never resolved against the agents the session can actually dispatch: (a) the plan skill's critique panel names 'Software Architect'/'Code Reviewer'/'Test Results Analyzer' — 'Code Reviewer' did not exist in the user's roster and the Task dispatch FAILED, costing a re-run; (b) the audit persona catalog ships frontmatter subagent_types ('Software Architect', 'Performance Benchmarker', 'Code Reviewer', ...) that don't exist in equipped repos, forcing the model to improvise a mapping; (c) dev-pick/council-batch recommend registry personas ('Frontend Developer', 'Backend Architect', 'Security Engineer', 'Data Engineer') instead of the repo's equipped agents in equipment.json; (d) build never preflights that the CLI-emitted subagent_types are actually registered — in a mono-repo session rooted above the equipped submodule, every equip-generated agent was undispatchable and the model degraded all ~12 dispatches to general-purpose with paraphrased rules. (Evidence items 18, 34, 38, 26.)
locations: dummyindex/skills/plan/SKILL.md:61-65 (critic table hardcodes the three global subagent_types; the general-purpose fallback at line 59 is only consulted AFTER a failed dispatch)
dummyindex/skills/audit/agents/architecture.md:6, correctness.md:6, maintainability.md:6, performance.md:6, security.md:6, data-integrity.md:6 (frontmatter subagent_type: global persona names)
dummyindex/context/domains/audit/catalog.py:51-59 (catalog emits frontmatter subagent_type verbatim; no resolution against the repo roster)
dummyindex/context/domains/council_batch.py:49-57,186 (CRITIC_ROSTER + architect hardcode 'Security Engineer'/'Data Engineer'/'Backend Architect')

dummyindex/context/domains/dev_pick.py:28-40 (SubagentType registry of agent-pack names; fallbacks ladder exists at lines 46-63 but the primary is never checked against installed agents or equipment.json)
dummyindex/skills/build/SKILL.md:22 (step 0 checks equipment.json exists but never that the emitted subagent_types are dispatchable in THIS session)
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED across four sites: every persona table assumes a specific end-user-global agent pack (the dev_pick docstring at lines 14-17 even asserts these are 'the exact end-user-global Claude agent names'), but no layer — CLI or skill — resolves a recommended subagent_type against (1) the repo's equipment.json or (2) the session's actually-registered agent list before dispatch. The dev-pick fallbacks array exists but is only documented for the council skill, and the audit/plan skills tell the model to fall back only after noticing unavailability, which in practice means after a failed Task call.
defects.proposed_fix: Skill-side (surgical, no CLI schema change): (1) plan SKILL.md step 7 — replace the hardcoded critic subagent_types with an explicit resolution instruction: 'before dispatching, check the available agent list in your environment; prefer the equipped agents from .context/equipment.json ({proj}-reviewer for risk, {label}-implementer for reuse/architecture, {label}-tester for testability) when present; otherwise use general-purpose with the inlined mandate — never dispatch a subagent_type you have not confirmed exists'; (2) audit SKILL.md step (line 48 area) — move the availability check BEFORE the first dispatch and add per-card fallbacks (frontmatter `fallbacks:` line, parsed by audit/catalog.py into PersonaCard — small additive field with default ()); (3) build SKILL.md step 0 — after the first --next-wave, verify each distinct subagent_type is dispatchable in this session; when one is not (e.g. agents live in a submodule's .claude that this session root does not register), warn once, Read the agent file, and inline its rules into a general-purpose prompt — exactly the manual workaround sessions already perform, now sanctioned and stated. CLI-side (DESIGN DECISION, can be a follow-up): teach dev-pick and council-batch to read .context/equipment.json when present and substitute the equipped implementer/reviewer for the matching registry persona, keeping the registry as fallback — keeps determinism, removes the agent-pack assumption.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/domains/audit/: extend parse_persona tests for the new fallbacks frontmatter field (absent -> ()). tests/context/domains/test_dev_pick.py: if the equipment-aware substitution is implemented, test_pick_dev_prefers_equipped_implementer (equipment.json with python-implementer -> subagent_type 'python-implementer', registry persona in fallbacks). Skill-content guard tests asserting plan/SKILL.md no longer contains a bare 'Code Reviewer' dispatch instruction without the availability-check sentence.

defects.symptom: council-batch --next leaks the raw Python enum repr in its dispatch contract for every dev-authored unit: '"subagent_type": "SubagentType.AI"' / '"SubagentType.FRONTEND"' / '"SubagentType.DATA"' in --json, and 'dev → SubagentType.DATA' in human output — the machine-readable batch is not directly usable for Task dispatch (the model had to call dev-pick per feature to get a real agent name). Reproduced at HEAD against a throwaway .context: next_batch unit serialized as "subagent_type": "SubagentType.DATA". (Evidence items 19's enum slice, 23.)
locations: dummyindex/context/domains/council_batch.py:165 (`subagent_type=str(pick.subagent_type)` — Enum.__str__ for a (str, Enum) mixin on Python >=3.11 is 'SubagentType.X', verified on this machine's 3.13: str(SubagentType.AI) == 'SubagentType.AI')
dummyindex/cli/council_batch.py:107 (human output prints the already-corrupted string)
dummyindex/context/domains/council_batch.py:186 (the architect path correctly uses SubagentType.BACKEND.value — the asymmetry confirms line 165 is the bug)
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED by execution: _dev_unit stringifies the SubagentType enum with str() instead of .value. Existing tests only assert truthiness for dev units (tests/context/domains/test_council_batch.py:192 `assert all(u.subagent_type ...)`), so the corrupted value passed CI.
defects.proposed_fix: One-line fix at council_batch.py:165: `subagent_type=pick.subagent_type.value` (DispatchUnit.subagent_type is typed str; .value is the wire-format agent name per the SubagentType docstring). No other call sites construct dev units. Tighten the lax test assertions while there.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/domains/test_council_batch.py: change/extend the stage-1 dev-unit test to assert the exact value (e.g. unit.subagent_type == 'Data Engineer' for a models/-file feature, and `not u.subagent_type.startswith('SubagentType.')` as a guard across all units). tests/context/domains/test_council_batch_cli.py: assert the --json payload's subagent_type values are real agent names, not enum reprs.

not_confirmed: Evidence item 0 (matcher never binds implementation items to the stack implementer; every impl item falls back to general-purpose): ALREADY FIXED at HEAD by commit d46fbc4 'fix(build-loop): match tasks to agents by capability lexicon, default to implementer' — mapping.py:186-196 routes any zero-score item to the manifest's implement-capable entry with fallback=False; reproduced ev0's item #9 text mapping to python-implementer. The residual mis-assignments are covered by defect 1.

Evidence item 2 (build silently ran general-purpose on an unequipped repo; plan didn't auto-equip): ALREADY FIXED — commit db341b6/fa7b07e added the _NOT_EQUIPPED_WARNING (cli/build_loop/waves.py:41-45) plus the `equipped` JSON flag (waves.py:158,169), build SKILL.md step 0 mandates STOP-and-warn, and plan SKILL.md step 9 auto-equips via `equip apply --for-proposal`.

Evidence item 31 (proposal-scoped equip missed the security capability despite RLS/tenant-isolation criticals): ALREADY FIXED in commit 9bb670b (v0.17.0) — constants.py:50-55 _PROPOSAL_CAPABILITY_TOKENS security row now carries 'rls'/'tenant'/'tenancy'/'isolation'/'rbac', and security is a templated generated specialist; reproduced extraction at HEAD ('Decide the RLS tenant-isolation model...' -> database+security). Note: the session that produced this evidence ran pre-0.17 code (it adopted registry 'Data Engineer' instead of generating a db specialist).

Evidence item 3's specific binding (docs item -> backend-reviewer): NOT REPRODUCIBLE at HEAD — the REVIEW lexicon is now only {review, lint} (mapping.py:60-62), and ev3's item text now routes to the implementer (via the 'mount' token / default rule) rather than the reviewer. The remaining docs-routing weakness is folded into defect 1.

Evidence item 26's submodule-registration half (agents written into frontend/.claude/agents aren't registered when the session roots at the mono parent): the dispatch-preflight/inline-fallback gap is covered in defect 6, but the underlying agent-registration/where-to-write question is C9-submodules territory — not investigated further here.

Evidence item 36's lifecycle half (no CLI verb to remove a single manifest entry) and items 27-30/35/37/44-48 (equip manifest lifecycle, rewrite-drops-items, grounded_in pointing at nonexistent files): C2-equip-manifest / C4-plugin-manager territory — only the stack-consistency cause of the garbage 'Frontend Developer' entry is handled here (defect 3).

Evidence items 16/19(frontier half)/21/22 (council-batch frontier is log-completeness-based, can't scope to drifted features): C3/C8 territory — only the enum-repr slice of items 19/23 is handled here (defect 7).

Evidence items 20 and 43 (plan has no quality gate of its own; critique-panel deferrals lacked user sign-off): plan-quality issues outside this cluster's dispatch-routing focus — not investigated here (no code claim to confirm/deny).
quick_wins: dummyindex/context/domains/council_batch.py:165 — change `str(pick.subagent_type)` to `pick.subagent_type.value` (1 line; fixes the 'SubagentType.AI'/'SubagentType.FRONTEND' leak in both --json and human output).

dummyindex/context/domains/buildloop/mapping.py:87 — remove 'comment' from the DOCS capability lexicon (1 line; stops docs specialists being matched to code tasks that mention a code comment — the module's own comment at lines 41-42 already forbids generic words).
